tasks.title: Add Call Earth action and Mars Throne structure with victory condition
tasks.description: Implement two connected features: (1) A 'Call Earth' action available when a unit stands on a Comm Dish, setting state.contactedEarth=true. (2) A 'Mars Throne' structure that becomes buildable after contacting Earth, consumes ALL remaining rocks and energy, is placed on an adjacent empty tile, and triggers a victory state. Includes canvas visuals, hotkeys, log entries, UI buttons, and victory messaging.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
1. 'Call Earth' button appears when unit is on a Comm Dish; sets state.contactedEarth=true.
2. 'Build Mars Throne' button only appears/enables after state.contactedEarth is true.
3. Building Mars Throne requires at least 1 rock and 1 energy.
4. Building Mars Throne consumes ALL rocks and ALL hovel energy.
5. Mars Throne is placed on an empty adjacent tile (no structure or unit).
6. A unique Mars Throne visual is drawn on the canvas.
7. A log entry announces construction.
8. A victory message is displayed: 'Elon has claimed Mars. Long live the King of Mars.'
9. Hotkey L for Call Earth and M for Build Mars Throne, both added to hotkey table.
10. Only one Mars Throne can be built per game (state.marsThronePlaced).
tasks.claudeMd: # Project Context You are implementing two connected features in a single-file vanilla JS browser game: `game.js` (with `index.html` and `style.css`). ## Tech Stack - Plain HTML/CSS/JS, no frameworks, no build tools, no npm - All game logic is in a single IIFE in `game.js` - Canvas-based rendering at 40x40px tiles - No tests exist ## Codebase Conventions ### State All game state lives in the `state` object (~line 68). Add new fields there AND in `init()` (~line 1479). ### Structure Pattern (follow exactly) Every structure follows this pattern: 1. `canBuildX()` — predicate returning boolean 2. `buildX()` — executes the build, pushes to `state.structures`, calls `addLog()` and `refreshView()` 3. `drawX(structure)` — canvas drawing function 4. Add case in `drawStructure()` (~line 273) to dispatch to your draw function 5. Add case in `updateTileInfo()` (~line 756) for the structure type string 6. Add button in `index.html` `#action-panel` div 7. Wire button with `addEventListener` in JS 8. Add hotkey in the `keydown` switch (~line 1422) 9. Add hotkey row in the hotkey table in `index.html` 10. Enable/disable button in `updateUI()` (~line 694) ### Key Existing Functions to Reuse - `findAdjacentOpenTile(row, col)` — finds adjacent tile with no unit and no structure. Returns `{row, col}` or null. Use this for Mars Throne placement. - `getStructureAt(row, col)` — returns structure at tile or null - `getTotalHovelEnergy()` — returns sum of all hovel energy - `getSelectedUnit()` — returns currently selected unit - `addLog(message, type)` — adds log entry. Types: 'build', 'construct', 'turn', 'energy', 'storm', 'explosion', 'degrade', 'gather', 'move' - `refreshView()` — re-renders canvas and updates UI ### Drawing Conventions - Each draw function takes a `structure` object with `.row`, `.col`, `.type` - Calculate pixel position: `var x = structure.col * TILE_SIZE; var y = structure.row * TILE_SIZE;` - Use `ctx` (the canvas 2d context) for all drawing - Use `TILE_SIZE` (40) for dimensions - Use `drawCircle(x, y, r)` helper for circles - End with a label: `ctx.fillStyle = '...'; ctx.font = 'bold Npx sans-serif'; ctx.textAlign = 'center'; ctx.fillText('LABEL', x + TILE_SIZE / 2, y + TILE_SIZE - 1);` ### Button Style in HTML All action buttons use class `action-btn` and follow this pattern: ```html ``` ## Implementation Steps ### Part 1: Call Earth Action 1. Add `contactedEarth: false` to `state` object (~line 82, after `hotkeyModalOpen`) 2. Add `contactedEarth: false` to `init()` (~line 1490) 3. Add `canCallEarth()` function: unit must be on a comm_dish, contactedEarth must be false, game not over 4. Add `callEarth()` function: sets `state.contactedEarth = true`, logs message, calls `refreshView()` 5. Add button in `index.html` after the comm dish button: `` 6. Wire button click handler (with gameOver guard) 7. Add hotkey `l` (lowercase) in keydown switch 8. Add hotkey row in hotkey table: `LCall Earth` 9. In `updateUI()`, enable/disable `call-earth-btn` based on `canCallEarth()` ### Part 2: Mars Throne 1. Add `marsThronePlaced: false` to `state` object 2. Add `marsThronePlaced: false` to `init()` 3. Add `canBuildMarsThrone()`: contactedEarth true, marsThronePlaced false, rocks >= 1, getTotalHovelEnergy() >= 1, findAdjacentOpenTile returns non-null for selected unit 4. Add `buildMarsThrone()`: consume all rocks (`state.resources.rocks = 0`), drain all hovels to 0 energy, find adjacent tile via findAdjacentOpenTile, push structure `{type: 'mars_throne', row, col}`, set marsThronePlaced=true, log build message, log victory message 'Elon has claimed Mars. Long live the King of Mars.', set state.gameOver=true 5. Add `drawMarsThrone(structure)`: Design a grand throne — wide stone base, tall rectangular back, golden crown/orb at top, label 'THRONE' in gold. Use warm gold (#FFD700), dark stone (#4a3a2a), and royal purple (#6a0dad) accents. 6. Add `mars_throne` case in `drawStructure()` 7. Add `mars_throne` case in `updateTileInfo()` → 'Mars Throne' 8. Add button in `index.html` after call-earth button: `` 9. Wire button click handler 10. Add hotkey `m` in keydown switch 11. Add hotkey row in hotkey table: `MBuild Mars Throne` 12. In `updateUI()`, enable/disable `build-throne-btn` based on `canBuildMarsThrone()` ### Part 3: Victory Styling 1. Add `.log-entry.victory` CSS class in `style.css` — gold color (#FFD700), bold, maybe with text-shadow 2. Use log type 'victory' for the victory message in buildMarsThrone ## Gotchas - `getTotalHovelEnergy()` returns total energy across all hovels. To drain ALL energy, iterate all structures and set energy=0 for each rock_hovel. - The game uses `var` everywhere (not `let`/`const` consistently) — match existing style per section. Newer additions use `var` in the structure functions. - `findAdjacentOpenTile` checks for both units AND structures, which is exactly what we need. - The `state.gameOver = true` line prevents all further actions. The victory state reuses this mechanism. - When adding to `updateUI()`, follow the existing pattern of getting the button element and setting `.disabled`. - The hotkey table in `index.html` is a static HTML table — add rows manually. - Make sure the Call Earth button only shows as enabled when standing on a Comm Dish AND contactedEarth is false. After calling, it should stay disabled forever. - Make sure Build Mars Throne button stays disabled until contactedEarth is true.

checklist.description: Add contactedEarth and marsThronePlaced state flags
steps: Add contactedEarth: false to state object after hotkeyModalOpen (~line 82)
Add marsThronePlaced: false to state object
Add contactedEarth: false to init() reset block (~line 1490)
Add marsThronePlaced: false to init() reset block
Verify state object has both new fields

checklist.description: Implement Call Earth action (canCallEarth + callEarth functions)
steps: Add canCallEarth() function: selected unit on comm_dish, contactedEarth is false
Add callEarth() function: set state.contactedEarth = true, addLog with message, refreshView()
Verify callEarth sets contactedEarth to true

checklist.description: Add Call Earth button in HTML, wire click handler and hotkey
steps: Add <button id='call-earth-btn'> in index.html #action-panel after comm dish button
Wire click handler in JS with gameOver guard
Add 'l' case in keydown switch calling callEarth()
Add hotkey row LCall Earth to hotkey table
In updateUI(), add call-earth-btn enable/disable based on canCallEarth()
Add canBuildMarsThrone() that checks: contactedEarth true, marsThronePlaced false, rocks >= 1, getTotalHovelEnergy() >= 1, findAdjacentOpenTile(unit.row, unit.col) returns non-null
Verify it returns false when contactedEarth is false
Verify it returns false when rocks are 0 or energy is 0
Verify it returns false when marsThronePlaced is true

checklist.description: Implement buildMarsThrone function with resource consumption and victory
steps: Add buildMarsThrone() that calls canBuildMarsThrone guard
Set state.resources.rocks = 0
Iterate all structures, set energy = 0 for each rock_hovel
Call findAdjacentOpenTile to get placement tile
Push {type: 'mars_throne', row, col} to state.structures
Set state.marsThronePlaced = true
Call addLog for build message (type: 'build')
Call addLog with 'Elon has claimed Mars. Long live the King of Mars.' (type: 'victory')
Set state.gameOver = true
Call refreshView()

checklist.description: Add Build Mars Throne button in HTML, wire click handler and hotkey
steps: Add <button id='build-throne-btn'> in index.html #action-panel after call-earth button
Wire click handler in JS with gameOver guard
Add 'm' case in keydown switch calling buildMarsThrone()
Add hotkey row for M / Build Mars Throne in hotkey table
In updateUI(), add build-throne-btn enable/disable based on canBuildMarsThrone()

checklist.description: Draw Mars Throne visual on canvas
steps: Add drawMarsThrone(structure) function with throne visual (stone base, tall back, golden crown, label 'THRONE')
Add 'mars_throne' case in drawStructure() dispatch
Verify the visual renders within the 40x40 tile bounds

checklist.description: Add Mars Throne to tile info display
steps: Add 'mars_throne' case to updateTileInfo() structure text block, displaying 'Mars Throne'
Verify clicking on Mars Throne tile shows correct info
Add .log-entry.victory CSS rule in style.css with gold color (#FFD700), bold text, optional text-shadow
Verify victory log entries appear styled differently from other logs

quality_checklist.description: No duplicate state initialization — contactedEarth and marsThronePlaced appear exactly once in state object and once in init()
steps: Grep for contactedEarth in game.js, verify exactly 2 initialization sites (state + init)
Grep for marsThronePlaced in game.js, verify exactly 2 initialization sites

quality_checklist.description: canBuildMarsThrone correctly prevents building with 0 resources
steps: Read canBuildMarsThrone, verify rocks >= 1 AND getTotalHovelEnergy() >= 1 checks exist
Verify marsThronePlaced check prevents double-build

quality_checklist.description: buildMarsThrone drains ALL resources, not a fixed amount
steps: Verify rocks are set to 0 (not decremented by a fixed amount)
Verify all hovels' energy is set to 0 (iterate structures, not drain a fixed amount)

quality_checklist.description: Code style matches existing patterns — var declarations, function naming, indentation
steps: Check new functions use var (not let/const) to match surrounding code in the structure-building section
Check function naming follows camelCase pattern: canBuildX, buildX, drawX
Check indentation matches 4 spaces used elsewhere

quality_checklist.description: All new buttons have proper disabled state management in updateUI()
steps: Verify call-earth-btn is disabled when canCallEarth() is false
Verify build-throne-btn is disabled when canBuildMarsThrone() is false
Verify no button is left permanently enabled or disabled

checks.description: Call Earth button appears when unit stands on Comm Dish
steps: Open the game in browser
Use browser console to give resources: manually set state to have a comm dish on the map
Move unit onto the Comm Dish tile
Verify 'Call Earth' button becomes enabled
Click Call Earth and verify log message appears

checks.description: Build Mars Throne button appears only after contacting Earth with sufficient resources
steps: After calling Earth, verify 'Build Mars Throne' button exists
Give the player at least 1 rock and 1 energy
Verify Build Mars Throne button becomes enabled
Verify with 0 rocks or 0 energy the button is disabled

checks.description: Building Mars Throne consumes all resources and shows victory
steps: Click Build Mars Throne with resources available
Verify rocks count goes to 0
Verify energy count goes to 0
Verify Mars Throne structure appears on canvas on an adjacent tile
Verify victory log message appears: 'Elon has claimed Mars. Long live the King of Mars.'
Verify game is over (no further actions possible)

checks.description: Hotkeys L and M work correctly
steps: Press L while on a Comm Dish to trigger Call Earth
Press M with sufficient resources to trigger Build Mars Throne
Open hotkey modal (ESC) and verify L and M are listed

checks.description: Only one Mars Throne can be built per game
steps: After building Mars Throne, verify game is over and no further building is possible
Verify state.marsThronePlaced is true
